subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Reference (Step 0 Audit)
- **Standard game** — 2 to 8 players.
- **Solo variant** — 1-player solitaire mode exists in the rulebook.

#### Tiles
- **Letter tiles** — 144 lowercase letter tiles stored in a banana-shaped zippered pouch.
- **Tile distribution** — Weighted by English letter frequency. Common letters (E, A, I, O) appear many times; rare letters (Q, Z, X, J) appear 1-2 times each.

#### Other Components
- **Banana pouch** — 1 zippered banana-shaped cloth pouch (doubles as storage and the draw pile container).
- **Rulebook** — 1 folded instruction sheet.

#### No Board
- **Play surface** — Each player uses the table space in front of them. There is no shared board.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### The Bunch
- **Central pool** — All 144 tiles are placed face-down in the center of the table. This pool is called the **Bunch**.
- **Mixing** — Shuffle and spread tiles face-down so no one can read them.

#### Player Areas
- **Personal workspace** — Each player claims a section of table space for building their own crossword grid.
- **No shared grid** — Players do not interact with each other's grids. Each grid is independent.

#### No Score Track
- **Race format** — There is no score track. The game ends when someone calls "Bananas!" and their grid is verified.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Starting Tile Count (by Player Count)
- **2-4 players** — Each player draws **21 tiles** face-down from the Bunch.
- **5-6 players** — Each player draws **15 tiles** face-down from the Bunch.
- **7-8 players** — Each player draws **11 tiles** face-down from the Bunch.

#### Tile Handling
- **Face-down draw** — Players draw tiles without looking at them.
- **Flip on signal** — Tiles remain face-down until the game begins.

#### Seating
- **Equal access** — All players should be within arm's reach of the Bunch or designate a central area everyone can access quickly.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Game Start
1. **"Split!"** — Any player calls out "Split!" and all players simultaneously flip their tiles face-up.
2. **Simultaneous play** — There are no turns. All players begin arranging tiles into their personal crossword grid at the same time.

#### Grid Rules (Apply Immediately)
- **Connected crossword** — All tiles must form a single connected crossword-style grid (words reading left-to-right and top-to-bottom).
- **Valid words only** — Every horizontal and vertical sequence of 2+ letters must be a valid English dictionary word.
- **No proper nouns** — Proper nouns, abbreviations, and acronyms are not allowed.
- **Rearrangement** — Players may rearrange their entire grid at any time during play.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### No Turns — Real-Time Play
- **Simultaneous** — All players work on their grids at the same time. There is no turn order.
- **Continuous action** — Players arrange, rearrange, Peel, and Dump at any moment without waiting.

#### Core Flow
1. **Build** — Arrange your tiles into a valid crossword grid.
2. **Peel** — When you use all your tiles, call "Peel!" Everyone (including you) draws 1 tile from the Bunch.
3. **Repeat** — Continue building. Each Peel adds 1 tile to every player's hand.
4. **Endgame trigger** — When the Bunch has fewer tiles than the number of players, the next person to use all their tiles calls "Bananas!" instead of "Peel!"

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Peel
- **Trigger** — You have placed all tiles into your grid with no tiles remaining in your hand.
- **Call** — Say "Peel!" loudly enough for all players to hear.
- **Effect** — Every player (including the caller) draws exactly 1 tile from the Bunch.
- **Mandatory** — All players must draw; you cannot decline a Peel.

#### Dump
- **Purpose** — Exchange an unwanted tile for new ones.
- **Action** — Place 1 tile face-down back into the Bunch and draw 3 tiles from the Bunch.
- **Net cost** — You end up with 2 more tiles than before (return 1, take 3).
- **No announcement** — You do not need to say "Dump" aloud. Other players do not draw.
- **Timing** — You may Dump at any time, as often as you want, as long as tiles remain in the Bunch.

#### Grid Rearrangement
- **Allowed** — You may completely tear apart and rebuild your grid at any time.
- **No penalty** — There is no cost for rearranging.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Bananas! (Winning Call)
- **Condition** — The Bunch has fewer tiles remaining than the number of players, and you have placed all your tiles into a valid grid.
- **Call** — Say "Bananas!" to signal a potential win.
- **Verification** — All other players stop and inspect the caller's grid.

#### Rotten Banana (Invalid Win)
- **If invalid** — If any word in the caller's grid is not a valid English word (or the grid is not fully connected), the caller has a "Rotten Banana."
- **Penalty** — The Rotten Banana player is **eliminated** from the game. Their tiles are returned face-down to the Bunch.
- **Game continues** — Remaining players resume play.

#### Word Validity Standards
- **Dictionary** — Any standard English dictionary agreed upon before the game. Typically the Official Scrabble Players Dictionary or similar.
- **Not allowed** — Proper nouns, abbreviations, hyphenated words, words requiring apostrophes.
- **Single letters** — A single letter standing alone is not a word.

#### Solo Variant
- **Setup** — Take 21 tiles from the Bunch.
- **Goal** — Use all 144 tiles in one connected grid.
- **Peel** — When all hand tiles are placed, draw 1 tile.
- **Timing** — Play against the clock for a personal best.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Endgame Trigger
- **Bunch depletion** — When the Bunch contains fewer tiles than the number of players, the game enters the endgame phase.
- **No more Peels** — "Peel" is no longer possible because there are not enough tiles for everyone to draw.

#### Winning
- **Bananas call** — First player to use all tiles in a valid connected crossword grid calls "Bananas!" and wins.
- **Verification required** — Other players check the grid. All words must be valid.
- **Instant win** — If the grid is valid, that player wins immediately.

#### Scoring Model
- **No points** — Bananagrams has no point scoring system.
- **Binary outcome** — You either win (valid Bananas) or you do not.
- **Elimination** — Players who call invalid Bananas are eliminated (Rotten Banana).

#### Tiebreaker
- **No ties** — Only one player can call Bananas first. If contested, the player who called first is verified first.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### Speed Over Perfection
- **Short words first** — Start with 2-3 letter words to place tiles quickly. Longer words can come later through rearrangement.
- **Common patterns** — Build around high-frequency short words: AT, AN, IN, IT, ON, IS, THE, AND.
- **Flexible layout** — Keep your grid loose with multiple connection points rather than one long chain.

#### Vowel-Consonant Balance
- **Monitor ratio** — Aim for roughly 40% vowels in your grid. If you accumulate too many consonants, consider a Dump.
- **Vowel connectors** — Words like A, I, and short vowel words help bridge consonant-heavy sections.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
#### Frequent Errors
- **Over-investing in long words** — Building elaborate long words locks tiles into positions that are hard to rearrange under pressure.
- **Ignoring invalid words** — In the rush, players accidentally create 2-letter sequences between words that are not valid (e.g., QI is valid, but QA is not).
- **Hoarding Dumps** — Refusing to Dump difficult letters (Q, Z, X without U or common partners) wastes time. The 3-tile penalty is often worth it.
- **Tunnel vision** — Focusing on one section while ignoring a pile of unplaced tiles. Periodically scan all remaining tiles.
- **Rushing Bananas** — Calling Bananas without carefully checking every word risks Rotten Banana elimination.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1: Dump or Struggle?
- **Dump** — If a tile has sat unused for 30+ seconds and you cannot see a placement, Dump it. Taking 3 tiles is better than stalling.
- **Struggle** — If you are close to using all tiles and the Bunch is nearly empty, avoid Dumping (net +2 tiles delays your finish).

#### Decision 2: Rebuild or Patch?
- **Rebuild** — If your grid is stuck with no connection points, tear it apart and start fresh. This is often faster than forcing bad placements.
- **Patch** — If only 1-2 tiles remain, look for ways to extend existing words or add short cross-words.

#### Decision 3: Peel Timing Awareness
- **Aggressive Peeling** — Finishing tiles quickly forces opponents to draw more tiles, increasing their workload.
- **Peel pressure** — Each Peel gives everyone 1 tile, but you called it because you were ready. Opponents may not be.
